methods.description: Fourier-transform infrared spectroscopy (FT-IR) was used to analyze the sample by recording its spectrum to identify chemical bonds and functional groups present.

steps.description: Approximately 1.0 mg of freeze-dried OM extracted from the sample was homogenized in 100 mg of spectroscopic-grade KBr and used to create a disc-like potassium bromide (KBr) pellet for analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare KBr pellet with sample for FT-IR analysis

steps.description: Background KBr spectra were obtained and spectra were compared to the background.
steps.searchable_summary: record and compare background KBr spectra

steps.description: Spectra were recorded by accumulating 256 scans in the 4000 to 400 cm−1 mid-infrared spectral range in the absorbance mode with a resolution of 4 cm−1.
steps.searchable_summary: record FT-IR spectra with specified parameters

steps.description: Baseline correction was conducted using the automatic baseline correction method.
steps.searchable_summary: apply automatic baseline correction to spectra

methods.description: High-resolution magic angle spinning nuclear magnetic resonance (HR-MAS NMR) was used to analyze the organic extract for detailed molecular information.

steps.description: Thoroughly dried organic extract (∼15 mg) was placed in a 4 mm zirconium oxide rotor and 1 mL D2O was added and titrated to pH 13.1 using NaOD.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare sample in rotor with D2O and NaOD for NMR

steps.description: 1H HR-MS NMR spectra were acquired using a Bruker 500 MHz Avance spectrometer.
Basic spectral assignments were verified by two-dimensional (2D) experiments such as HSQC and TOCSY.
steps.searchable_summary: verify spectral assignments with 2D NMR experiments

methods.description: A method to extract different compounds from a sample using a series of solvents to separate polar and non-polar components.

steps.description: Powdered sample (15 g) was sonicated twice for 15 min, each time with 30 mL double-distilled water to remove highly polar water-soluble compounds.
steps.searchable_summary: sonicate sample with water to extract polar compounds

steps.description: Water extracts were centrifuged at 1048 xg for 30 min, decanted, and the combined extracts freeze-dried.
The water extracted residue was freeze-dried and then extracted with methanol, dichloromethane:methanol (1:1; v/v), and dichloromethane.
steps.searchable_summary: extract residue with methanol and dichloromethane

methods.description: Derivatization of solvent extracts to form trimethylsilyl (TMS) derivatives followed by gas chromatography-mass spectrometry (GC-MS) analysis to identify individual components.

steps.description: Dried solvent extracts were redissolved in 500 µL of dichloromethane:methanol (1:1; v/v) and aliquots of the reconstituted extracts (50 µL) were evaporated to dryness under nitrogen atmosphere.
steps.searchable_summary: redissolve and dry solvent extracts under nitrogen

steps.description: The dried residues were converted to TMS derivatives using BSTFA and pyridine, incubated for 2 h at 70 °C.
GC-MS analysis of the derivatized extracts was carried out using an Agilent model 6890 N gas chromatograph.
steps.searchable_summary: perform GC-MS analysis on TMS derivatives

methods.description: GC-MS analysis of silylated lipid extracts to identify and quantify lipid components in the sample.

steps.description: GC-MS analysis of silylated lipid extracts was performed to identify major classes of compounds.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze silylated lipid extracts using GC-MS
